title: anil tinctoria var. orthocarpa
intro: Anil tinctoria var. orthocarpa, also known as the 'Alpine Madder', is a perennial plant species within the genus Rubia, native to the mountainous regions of the Indian subcontinent and the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Anil tinctoria var. orthocarpa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the treatment of various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its root is used to create natural dyes and pigments.
